jokes.text: Are you pickling squirrel meat? Because I can lend you my skull presser.
jokes.setup: Kenneth offers to help with containers, assumes normal organization purpose
jokes.punchline: Immediately jumps to bizarre squirrel meat pickling with casual offer of skull presser
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth character moment - innocent tone with absolutely deranged content. 'Skull presser' is hilariously specific and disturbing.

jokes.text: There's a stacker thing to separate your junk mail from your humidifier catalogs.
jokes.setup: Liz describing organization containers to fix her life
jokes.punchline: Hyper-specific categorization includes 'humidifier catalogs' as if that's normal
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about organization culture's absurd specificity. Character-appropriate neurotic detail.

jokes.text: A round, plastic deal that holds your shoes with a pocket for a photograph of what shoes are in there.
jokes.setup: Continuing description of organization products
jokes.punchline: Container needs a photo to identify shoes that are literally inside it
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity of organization products. Logic so circular it's funny.

jokes.text: Like a phoenix rise... Watch out! Or... maybe this is going to be the worst day ever.
jokes.setup: Liz delivers inspirational phoenix metaphor
jokes.punchline: Immediately interrupted by physical mishap, instantly reverses outlook
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom structure - grand statement immediately undercut. Perfect Liz Lemon deflation.

jokes.text: Everybody shut up! Shut up, Lutz.
jokes.setup: Liz calling morning meeting
jokes.punchline: Pre-emptively tells Lutz to shut up before he even speaks
jokes.explanation: Efficient character beat showing Liz's frayed state and Lutz's reputation. Economy of language perfect.

jokes.text: Already today, I have lost faith in 'decorganizing'
jokes.setup: Liz listing her morning disasters
jokes.punchline: Creates portmanteau 'decorganizing' referencing earlier organization obsession
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that calls back to opening sequence. Shows writing team's attention to detail.

jokes.text: lost a shoving match to what I thought was a female bike messenger
jokes.setup: Continuing list of morning disasters with 'shoving match'
jokes.punchline: 'What I thought was' implies misidentification, probably a small man
jokes.explanation: Misdirection works well. Implies Liz lost to someone even less intimidating than expected.

jokes.text: Why are you not wearing pants?... They still have the heat set for winter and my office is boiling.
jokes.setup: Reasonable question about missing pants
jokes.punchline: Mundane temperature explanation for inappropriate workplace attire
jokes.explanation: Visual absurdity treated casually. Frank's logic is reasonable but inappropriate for workplace.

jokes.text: I told you to buy more 'Proactiv'.
jokes.setup: Cerie claims Liz told her to be 'proactive'
jokes.punchline: Liz meant the acne medication, not the business term
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay revealing character dynamics. Liz's shallow priorities vs Cerie's misunderstanding.

jokes.text: Sir, as I'm sure you know from reading my blog
jokes.setup: Jenna addressing NBC executive about St. Patrick's Day disaster
jokes.punchline: Assumes executive reads her blog, shows narcissistic delusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna character moment - assumes everyone follows her personal life closely.

jokes.text: inspired by, but, for legal reasons, not based on Janis Joplin
jokes.setup: Describing her film project
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous legal distinction that fools no one
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about Hollywood's transparent attempts to avoid copyright issues.

jokes.text: Synonym's just another word for the word you want to use...?
jokes.setup: Jenna singing from her Janis Joplin parody
jokes.punchline: Misunderstands 'Freedom' lyric, creates nonsensical grammar lesson
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misunderstanding of famous lyric shows Jenna's intellectual limitations. Wordplay works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: You and this gravy-face have slarneyed up a real donnybrook!
jokes.setup: Irish executive expressing anger
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top Irish dialect with made-up words
jokes.explanation: Funny Irish stereotyping with invented words. 'Slarneyed' and 'gravy-face' are memorably absurd.

jokes.text: Wake up, mother --
jokes.setup: Tracy hosting St. Patrick's Day parade coverage
jokes.punchline: Starts to curse on live TV before cutting himself off
jokes.explanation: Sets up the central conflict about Tracy cursing on TV. Classic comedy timing with cutoff.

jokes.text: 50 grand? Can anyone cut this in half?
jokes.setup: Learning about $50,000 FCC fine
jokes.punchline: Treats major fine like splitting a restaurant check
jokes.explanation: Shows Tracy's wealth disconnect - massive fine treated casually. Efficient character revelation.

jokes.text: I only heard the 'thin' part, Liz.
jokes.setup: Liz tells Jenna she's spreading herself too thin
jokes.punchline: Jenna only processes the compliment about being thin
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna vanity moment. Selective hearing focused only on appearance compliment. Excellent character writing.

jokes.text: Obviously, you can't do both T.G.S. and Janie Jimplin.
jokes.setup: Jack addressing Jenna's scheduling conflict
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'Janis Joplin' as 'Janie Jimplin'
jokes.explanation: Shows Jack's dismissive attitude toward Jenna's project through mispronunciation. Character-appropriate condescension.

jokes.text: We could cut the lesbian scene. But the Oscars love that kind of thing. There's two guys at my gym named Oscar.
jokes.setup: Jenna considering cutting scenes, mentions Oscars love lesbian content
jokes.punchline: Reveals she thinks 'Oscars' refers to actual people named Oscar
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misunderstanding showing Jenna's complete disconnect from industry knowledge. Perfect character stupidity.

jokes.text: Jenna. That's a glue stick.
jokes.setup: Scene of Jenna applying something to her lips
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's using office supplies as makeup
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing Jenna's exhausted state. Simple but effective physical comedy.

jokes.text: The pocket microwave? You can buy it on Friday along with everyone else. It has a ham button. You used my idea!
jokes.setup: Jack hints at big microwave project
jokes.punchline: Reveals absurdly specific 'ham button' that Liz apparently suggested
jokes.explanation: Sets up the microwave subplot while showing Liz's weird food obsessions. Ham button is memorably specific.

jokes.text: I haven't been this tired since I was forced to do that dance marathon in Dubai.
jokes.setup: Jenna complaining about exhaustion to doctor
jokes.punchline: Casually references being 'forced' into dance marathon in Dubai
jokes.explanation: Dark implication delivered casually. Shows Jenna's bizarre past and current obliviousness to trauma.

jokes.text: Mostly because I checked the wrong box on a form once.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman explaining his sleep research specialization
jokes.punchline: Entire medical specialty was accidental bureaucratic error
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Spaceman moment - medical incompetence delivered with complete confidence. Classic character writing.

jokes.text: It's being funded by the U.S. military and the W.N.B.A.
jokes.setup: Describing military funding for sleep prevention research
jokes.punchline: Unexpected pairing with women's basketball league
jokes.explanation: Unexpected pairing jokes about WNBA's struggle with viewership. Misdirection works well.

jokes.text: Take 25 of these a day for the rest of your life.
jokes.setup: Offering experimental sleep prevention pills
jokes.punchline: Absurdly high dosage for lifetime commitment
jokes.explanation: Typical Dr. Spaceman reckless prescribing. Dark comedy about medical irresponsibility.

jokes.text: I've got my Princess Leia outfit and some Playgirl magazines from the early 1980s. They will dismiss me immediately as a weirdo.
jokes.setup: Liz planning to get out of jury duty
jokes.punchline: Hyper-specific weird combination designed to seem unfit
jokes.explanation: Shows Liz's calculated weirdness. Specific vintage Playgirl detail makes it funnier than generic porn.

jokes.text: We all will, Kenneth. We all will.
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking who will yell at him for lunch order mistakes
jokes.punchline: Writers ominously promise collective abuse
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about workplace dynamics. Kenneth's innocence contrasted with writers' cruelty.

jokes.text: You guys are my best friends.
jokes.setup: Writers promise to collectively yell at Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Kenneth interprets abuse as friendship
jokes.explanation: Perfectly tragic Kenneth moment. His desperate need for connection makes abuse feel like friendship.

jokes.text: I guess 'F.C.C.' stands for 'Federal Bunch of Sticklers'.
jokes.setup: Tracy complaining about FCC fine
jokes.punchline: Creates alternate meaning that's more petulant than official
jokes.explanation: Shows Tracy's childish worldview - regulatory agency becomes personal antagonist. Character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I learned that if you pay some money afterwards, you can say whatever you want on TV.
jokes.setup: Tracy claiming to have learned lesson from fine
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong lesson that money enables anything
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - learns opposite of intended lesson. Satirizes wealthy entitlement effectively.

jokes.text: That's always the lesson! If you have money, you can do whatever you want.
jokes.setup: Tracy defending his interpretation
jokes.punchline: States uncomfortable truth about wealth and consequences
jokes.explanation: Dark social commentary delivered through Tracy's childlike honesty. Uncomfortable but accurate observation.

jokes.text: I am not asking you this as the boss you love to undermine, but as the friend whose birthday you love to forget.
jokes.setup: Liz asking Frank to behave normally
jokes.punchline: Two specific examples of Frank's personal failures
jokes.explanation: Great structure building from professional to personal grievances. Shows Liz's exasperation effectively.

jokes.text: And I don't really think it's fair for me to be on a jury because I'm a hologram.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to get out of jury duty with various excuses
jokes.punchline: Claims to be a hologram as if that's a reasonable excuse
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of jury duty excuses. Hologram claim is memorably bizarre and quotable.

jokes.text: The subject is unusually thirsty.
jokes.setup: Jenna apparently drinking from toilets
jokes.punchline: Clinical understatement of extreme behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Spaceman deadpan observation. Medical detachment from obviously alarming behavior.

jokes.text: College students, the morbidly obese and homosexuals.
jokes.setup: Jack describing marketing target demographics
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific and inappropriate grouping
jokes.explanation: Shows Jack's corporate thinking and casual offensiveness. Weird demographic combinations.

jokes.text: the 'Bitenuker', is highly offensive to those who speak either French or Dutch.
jokes.setup: Microwave name had to be changed due to offense
jokes.punchline: Hyper-specific linguistic offense to two random languages
jokes.explanation: Absurd corporate legal concern. 'Bitenuker' sounds vaguely dirty, making concern funnier.

jokes.text: A Franco-Dutchman would pronounce it 'bet nyooker'. Hey! That's awful!
jokes.setup: Explaining pronunciation to Franco-Dutch employee
jokes.punchline: She's personally offended in real time
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff. Having the affected person right there makes the offense immediate and funnier.

jokes.text: Let's not shoot for the middle this time.
jokes.setup: Jack motivating writers to brainstorm microwave names
jokes.punchline: Implies TGS deliberately aims for mediocrity
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about the show's quality while maintaining character voice. Jack's corporate speak.

jokes.text: This ain't Chicago, honey.
jokes.setup: Liz's voice disguise used to work in Chicago
jokes.punchline: Implies New Yorkers are less easily fooled
jokes.explanation: Regional rivalry joke. Court officer's deadpan delivery makes simple observation funnier.

jokes.text: The pig spoor has hit the wind spinner.
jokes.setup: Kenneth reporting chaos to Liz
jokes.punchline: Rural euphemism for 'shit hit the fan'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth-ism. Perfect rural equivalent of common phrase, maintaining his innocent vocabulary.

jokes.text: And I keep starting to sneeze... but nothing comes out.
jokes.setup: Kenneth listing problems during chaos
jokes.punchline: Personal physical complaint amid workplace disasters
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's priorities showing personal discomfort equal to workplace chaos. Character-appropriate innocence.

jokes.text: Attention, everyone! All menstruating women go home immediately!
jokes.setup: Frank declaring himself in charge
jokes.punchline: First executive decision is bizarrely inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Shows Frank's complete unsuitability for leadership. Absurd first priority reveals character perfectly.

jokes.text: You know what I like to do for eight hours? The TV Guide crossword puzzle.
jokes.setup: Observing patient's eight-hour behavior
jokes.punchline: Doctor's idea of eight-hour activity is minimal puzzle
jokes.explanation: Dr. Spaceman's lazy approach to everything. TV Guide crossword implies extremely low intellectual engagement.

jokes.text: They're all dead, Lemon. They're all dead unless they come up with a name for my pocket microwave.
jokes.setup: Liz asking where everyone is
jokes.punchline: Jack's casual death threat over product naming
jokes.explanation: Jack's corporate priorities over human life. Dark comedy delivered with business-like casualness.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm rapping Obama. Hey, I'm Josh doing an impression of DeNiro as an auctioneer.
jokes.setup: Jack dismissing TV show writing as easy
jokes.punchline: Reduces complex comedy to lazy celebrity impressions
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on lazy comedy writing through Jack's dismissive examples. Shows his ignorance of the craft.

jokes.text: The Small Wonder. The 'Micro-Mate'. The 'Porta-Hotty'.
jokes.setup: Liz suggesting microwave names
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate sexual implications
jokes.explanation: Escalating sexual innuendo in product names. 'Porta-Hotty' is the obvious punchline building from the series.

jokes.text: I see you... self-publishing your novel and moving back in with your parents.
jokes.setup: Jack warning about TGS cancellation consequences
jokes.punchline: Specific humiliating failure scenario for Liz
jokes.explanation: Jack knows exactly what would devastate Liz - artistic and personal failure combined. Character-specific threat.

jokes.text: What sounds more shocking? Or...
jokes.setup: Tracy testing different curse combinations
jokes.punchline: Implied profanity comparison for shock value
jokes.explanation: Shows Tracy's approach to controversy as calculated rather than spontaneous. Functional setup.

jokes.text: All we do is joke around together about our stupid boss, Liz Lem...
jokes.setup: Tracy claiming to care about crew
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals they mock Liz, catches himself mid-name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy moment - accidentally reveals crew dynamics while talking to the boss. Good structure.

jokes.text: I'll just buy up all the ads and let that hilarious Tracy Jordan character do whatever he wants.
jokes.setup: Tracy suggesting becoming advertiser to avoid restrictions
jokes.punchline: Refers to himself in third person as 'that hilarious Tracy Jordan character'
jokes.explanation: Meta moment where Tracy sees himself as a character. Shows his disconnect from reality while setting up plot.

jokes.text: They knew what a 'Hot Richard' was?
jokes.setup: Learning his microwave name suggestion was rejected
jokes.punchline: Surprise that his sexual euphemism was recognized
jokes.explanation: Good Frank moment showing his crude humor and naivety

jokes.text: 'V'. 'A'. 'G'... Why don't we start over?
jokes.setup: Using Scrabble tiles to randomly name microwave
jokes.punchline: Accidentally spelling inappropriate word, quickly aborts
jokes.explanation: Classic setup where random chance creates inappropriate word. Jack's quick retreat is perfectly corporate.

jokes.text: Throw in a late Hannukah and the fact that I work in a Kwanzaa neighborhood
jokes.setup: Liz (as witness) describing holiday stress at work
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous religious holiday complications
jokes.explanation: Escalating holiday stress excuse. 'Kwanzaa neighborhood' pushes into absurdist territory.

jokes.text: But I suspect they were doing sex with each other.
jokes.setup: Describing employees' tardiness excuse
jokes.punchline: Awkward phrasing 'doing sex' instead of 'having sex'
jokes.explanation: Liz's awkward witness persona uses stilted language. 'Doing sex' is memorably weird phrasing.

jokes.text: most of whom would be unemployable in any other field
jokes.setup: Describing supervision of employees
jokes.punchline: Casual admission employees are fundamentally incompetent
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about workplace reality. Liz's brutal honesty about her staff under oath.

jokes.text: And don't even get me started on Tracy and Jenna.
jokes.setup: Liz complaining about difficult employees in fake testimony
jokes.punchline: Names the actual Tracy and Jenna from her real job
jokes.explanation: Meta moment where Liz's fake testimony becomes real venting. Blurs line between character and witness role.

jokes.text: All of it would dance in the warm mouth of my fire.
jokes.setup: Liz's fake arsonist testimony building to dramatic climax
jokes.punchline: Overwrought poetic language about destruction
jokes.explanation: Liz's dramatic performance as disturbed arsonist. 'Warm mouth of my fire' is memorably purple prose.

jokes.text: And a new, better, wonderful me would rise from the ashes like a phoenix.
jokes.setup: Continuing arsonist testimony about rebirth
jokes.punchline: Directly callbacks to opening phoenix metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback structure tying fake testimony to Liz's real morning hopes. Phoenix metaphor comes full circle.

jokes.text: So, it's kind of like a 'funcooker'.
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing pocket microwave oven
jokes.punchline: Innocent combination creates sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Episode's title joke and climactic moment. Kenneth's innocent wordplay accidentally creates perfect dirty name. Absolutely quotable.

jokes.text: One hug, you mean?
jokes.setup: Jack saying he owes Kenneth one
jokes.punchline: Kenneth assumes debt is emotional rather than professional
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment showing his need for affection over professional advancement. Character-driven misdirection.

jokes.text: Peace and love, Track Jordan.
jokes.setup: Reading Tracy's official apology
jokes.punchline: Misspells Tracy's name as 'Track'
jokes.explanation: Simple name mistake joke. Shows how little attention is paid to Tracy despite his self-importance.

jokes.text: I am an advertiser.
jokes.setup: Tracy undermining his own apology on live TV
jokes.punchline: Simple declaration of his scheme to avoid consequences
jokes.explanation: Tracy's simple solution to complex problem. Childlike logic delivered with confidence.

jokes.text: I'm freer than you. I'm freer than you.
jokes.setup: Liz getting dismissed from jury duty
jokes.punchline: Claims freedom while clearly heading to more work stress
jokes.explanation: Ironic triumph that audience knows is false. Liz's brief moment of superiority before reality hits.

jokes.text: Professor Bananas is dead.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman reporting on experimental results
jokes.punchline: Casually announces death of lab subject with absurd name
jokes.explanation: Dr. Spaceman's casual announcement of death with ridiculous subject name. Dark comedy delivered deadpan.

jokes.text: Hey. Is your vision steadily narrowing down to a pinpoint as if a darkness is closing in on you?
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman checking on Jenna's condition
jokes.punchline: Describes terrifying symptom as casual medical inquiry
jokes.explanation: Perfectly disturbing Dr. Spaceman moment. Describes obvious medical emergency as routine question.

jokes.text: Sleep or die!
jokes.setup: Liz trying to force Jenna to sleep before show
jokes.punchline: Ultimatum delivered while physically wrestling
jokes.explanation: Liz's desperate attempt at caring becomes aggressive. Physical comedy with darkly funny ultimatum.

jokes.text: Check out my funcooker!
jokes.setup: Tracy on live TV promoting during chaos
jokes.punchline: Uses Kenneth's innocent term as obvious sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback where Tracy uses Kenneth's innocent word for obvious innuendo on live TV. Peak episode moment.

jokes.text: My wife is throwing away some of our old towels. Do you want them? Because they're out by the trash cans.
jokes.setup: Tracy doing improvised TV commercial
jokes.punchline: Offers trash to America as advertisement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anti-commercial. Tracy's wealth makes him offer literal garbage as if it's valuable. Perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Here comes the funcooker.
jokes.setup: Tracy continuing his chaotic TV appearance
jokes.punchline: Returns to sexual innuendo with ominous delivery
jokes.explanation: Escalates the funcooker running gag. Tracy's delivery makes innocent word sound threatening.

jokes.text: Bite nuker!
jokes.setup: Liz suddenly remembering something during chaos
jokes.punchline: Recalls the original offensive microwave name
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier legal problem. Shows Liz's mind working even during crisis.

jokes.text: Let's go out this weekend and talk about you.
jokes.setup: Jenna addressing Liz after crisis ends
jokes.punchline: Fake friendship offer that's actually about herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment disguising narcissism as friendship. 'Talk about you' obviously means talk about herself.

jokes.text: All I did was ask for a Diet Slice and some pita chips.
jokes.setup: Everyone terrified of Liz after her courtroom performance
jokes.punchline: Innocent snack request treated as threat
jokes.explanation: Shows aftermath of Liz's scary courthouse performance. Mundane request now seems sinister to others.

jokes.text: heat up some ham... in the shower.
jokes.setup: Jack suggesting they both relax at home
jokes.punchline: Bizarre ham preparation method reveals product design
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd ending showing the microwave actually works in shower. Combines ham obsession with impossible product.

jokes.text: It works in the shower?
jokes.setup: Jack mentions heating ham in shower
jokes.punchline: Liz's amazement at impossible product feature
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction to absurd information. Liz's wonder at impossible engineering.

jokes.text: You know what? This really is the best day ever.
jokes.setup: After learning microwave works in shower
jokes.punchline: Reverses opening 'worst day ever' despite nothing improving
jokes.explanation: Perfect bookend callback. Shower microwave somehow makes everything worthwhile for Liz. Great circular structure.

jokes.text: Here comes the funcooker!
jokes.setup: Episode ending on high note
jokes.punchline: Liz enthusiastically adopts Kenneth's innocent sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending callback. Innocent Kenneth's word becomes episode catchphrase. Liz's enthusiasm for sexual innuendo is the cherry on top.
